name: Chocolate Kahlúa Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Chocolate Crust: Mix the crust ingredients and press onto the bottom and halfway up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Freeze for 5 minutes.
2. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 10 minutes.
3. Cheesecake: Prepare the chocolate crust. In a microwave-safe bowl, place 3/4 cup of melted chocolate chunks. Microwave on HIGH for 1 minute or until the chunks are melted, stirring occasionally. Add 1/4 cup of whipping cream and 1/4 cup of Kahlúa, stirring until blended.
4. In a mixer bowl, beat the cream cheese and sugar until fluffy. Add cocoa; beat until blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Stir in vanilla extract and the reserved chocolate mixture. Pour over the prepared crust.
6. Bake for 10 minutes at 400°F (200°C). Decrease to 275°F (140°C) and bake for an additional 45 minutes.
7. Remove from the oven and let cool. Loosen the cake from the rim of the pan and remove it carefully.
8. In a microwave-safe bowl, place the remaining melted chocolate chunks. Microwave on HIGH for 1 minute or until melted, stirring occasionally. Stir in 1/4 cup of whipping cream and 2 tbsp of Kahlúa; blend well.
9. Spread over the cake. Refrigerate for 6 hours before serving.
10. At serving time, beat the remaining whipped cream and powdered sugar until stiff. Garnish with rosettes of whipped cream.
11. Refrigerate any leftover cake.
